title: Capability-first routing across Excel target environments
originSignal: A request asks whether Excel automation, Power Query, VBA, or a workbook can run on a named platform, Office version, bitness, offline host, or recipient machine.
riskPattern: Routing directly to implementation can mistake structural evidence for runtime support, transfer evidence between different machines, or send DAX formula compatibility questions to general environment diagnostics.
regressionChecks: Route platform, host, runtime, support, and availability questions to office-environment-diagnostics before implementation.
Require a capability probe or captured capability evidence before making platform-specific automation claims.
Keep DAX and Power Pivot formula or function compatibility with power-pivot-dax-modeling when no platform-host runtime question is present.
Record structural evidence, runtime capability evidence, and workbook behavior evidence as separate levels.
Keep the execution environment separate from the authoring, automation, consumer, and recipient target environments.
expectedEvidence: Router output selects office-environment-diagnostics for an explicit platform/runtime question.
Router output preserves power-pivot-dax-modeling for DAX formula/function compatibility.
Compatibility report identifies capability status, evidence level, operation readiness, target environment, and unmet explicit requirements.
Structural evidence is never presented as runtime capability evidence or workbook behavior evidence.
boundaries: This sanitized specification contains no private workbook and does not prove that Excel is installed or usable on any machine.
A synthetic capability probe proves report behavior only; Windows runtime capability evidence requires a live local probe.
A successful local probe does not prove recipient readiness or workbook business correctness.
nextLiveWorkbookRequirement: Capture a runtime capability probe on the intended Windows automation machine, then validate a sanitized representative workbook in every distinct recipient target environment that matters.
